display.purpose: The single glance value per view — lap delta, balance verdict. Rare.

data.purpose: Geist Mono, tabular figures — every runtime number, time, delta, slider value.

label.purpose: Functional uppercase field labels and units; never decorative section eyebrows.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Popovers, menus, dialogs — the only genuinely floating layers.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus and active selection on interactive elements.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and value changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one 'act here' action per context. Mineral teal fill, dark ink. Used sparingly.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.155 0.020 235); background: oklch(0.800 0.115 192); border: none; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.720 0.130 193); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.800 0.115 192 / 0.25); } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: oklch(0.620 0.090 193); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: The workhorse action in a dense tool: surface fill, full border, ink text.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.965 0.004 230); background: oklch(0.195 0.008 235); border: 1px solid oklch(0.300 0.012 235); border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 160ms; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.235 0.010 235); border-color: oklch(0.360 0.014 235); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.800 0.115 192 / 0.25); }

components.name: Status Pill (Caution)
components.description: Semantic state tag. Tints background + text toward one data color at low alpha, always with a word — never color alone.
components.css: .ds-pill-caution { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.820 0.150 80); background: oklch(0.820 0.150 80 / 0.10); border: 1px solid oklch(0.820 0.150 80 / 0.35); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 8px; } .ds-pill-caution .ds-pill-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: oklch(0.820 0.150 80); }

components.name: Panel
components.description: Resting container: surface on the base floor, 1px border, flat (no shadow at rest). Nested cards forbidden.
components.html:
Aero Balance

Front wing 6 of 50 — try +2 for rotation on entry.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: oklch(0.195 0.008 235); border: 1px solid oklch(0.300 0.012 235); border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px 18px; } .ds-panel-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.965 0.004 230); } .ds-panel-body { margin: 0; font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; color: oklch(0.720 0.010 232); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Inset well, 1px border; focus shifts border to teal with a ring. Numeric entry uses mono.
components.css: .ds-input { font-family: Geist Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: oklch(0.965 0.004 230); background: oklch(0.125 0.005 235); border: 1px solid oklch(0.300 0.012 235); border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; transition: border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.800 0.115 192); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.800 0.115 192 / 0.25); }

components.name: Mode Switch
components.description: Top-level identity control. Tuner and Race Control as peers; active segment marked by teal text + a 2px teal underline, not a filled block.
components.css: .ds-modeswitch { display: inline-flex; gap: 4px; } .ds-mode { position: relative; font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.720 0.010 232); background: none; border: none; padding: 8px 12px 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-mode:hover { color: oklch(0.965 0.004 230); } .ds-mode.is-active { color: oklch(0.800 0.115 192); } .ds-mode.is-active::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 12px; right: 12px; bottom: 0; height: 2px; border-radius: 1px; background: oklch(0.800 0.115 192); }

components.name: Data Readout
components.description: The signature atom: stacked uppercase label + tabular mono value, optional semantic state color and track. Every timing row, setup slider, and stat is a density variant of this.
components.css: .ds-readout { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 3px; } .ds-readout-label { font-family: Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.560 0.012 234); } .ds-readout-value { font-family: Geist Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: oklch(0.965 0.004 230); line-height: 1.1; } .ds-readout-value.is-good { color: oklch(0.800 0.160 152); } .ds-readout-value.is-danger { color: oklch(0.640 0.205 27); }

narrative.overview: BoxBox looks like the screen a race engineer actually reads — graphite housing, data lit from within, one mineral teal glow marking what matters right now. It is an instrument, not a dashboard-as-decoration, built for two attention budgets at once: a driver glancing at a second monitor mid-lap, and a steward giving a race-control station full focus. The craft target is sim-HUD purpose executed with Linear / Vercel discipline. Color is rationed: a single teal brand accent for brand, selection, and 'act here,' and a separate functional data layer that means something specific every time. Numbers live in a tabular mono so columns lock; motion is responsive, never choreographed.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black graphite surfaces with a faint cool tint — the housing, not the subject.
One teal-cyan brand accent, used on ≤10% of any screen.
A disciplined semantic data palette, always paired with shape/text/position (never hue alone).
Tabular mono for every number; proportional grotesque for everything else.
Dense by design, calm under pressure; motion that reports state and nothing else.

rules.name: The One Teal Rule
rules.body: Teal appears on ≤10% of any screen. It is brand, selection, and 'act here' — nothing else. The moment two unrelated things are teal, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The Meaning-Not-Mood Rule
rules.body: A data color may only appear when its specific meaning is true. Green is never 'a nice green here'; it is good/fastest/gain. If a color isn't reporting a state, it's neutral graphite.

rules.name: The Redundant-Encoding Rule
rules.body: No state is ever signaled by hue alone. Every colored signal also carries text, a glyph, a position, or a shape, so it survives a color-blind read and a washed-out stream feed.

rules.name: The Tabular-Always Rule
rules.body: Every digit that can change at runtime uses the mono face with tabular-nums. Numbers must never reflow horizontally as they tick.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are a fixed rem scale, never fluid clamp(). A heading that shrinks inside a panel looks broken, not responsive.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Resting surfaces cast no shadow. Separate panels with a border and a tonal step; shadow is reserved for things that actually float.

dos: Do keep teal to ≤10% of any screen — brand, selection, and 'act here' only.
Do pair every colored state with text, a glyph, or position, so it survives a color-blind read and a washed-out stream.
Do render every runtime number in Geist Mono with tabular-nums so columns lock and values don't jitter.
Do separate panels with a 1px border and a tonal step; keep resting surfaces flat.
Do reserve a data color for its one specific meaning — green is good/fastest, amber is caution, never decoration.
Do tune density per mode (Race Control tighter, Tuner roomier) while keeping one component vocabulary.
donts: Don't use neon rainbow accents, carbon-fiber textures, angular racing-stripe decoration, or any gamer-RGB move.
Don't fall into the generic SaaS dashboard: no endless identical card grids, no pastel palette, no big-number hero-metric template.
Don't render skeuomorphic gauges — no chrome dials, photoreal tachometers, or glossy 3D bezels.
Don't ship cluttered overlay spam: everything on screen needs a hierarchy and a reason to be there.
Don't nest cards inside cards — use dividers and tonal insets.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text, fluid clamp() type, or decorative motion that doesn't report a state.
Don't signal anything by hue alone.
